# PlantUML Reference Materials

Quick reference guides and cheat sheets for PlantUML diagramming.

## Available References

### 1. Quick Reference (`quick-reference.md`)

**Purpose**: Fast lookup for common PlantUML syntax  
**Best for**: 
- Quick syntax lookups during diagram creation
- Learning PlantUML basics
- Command-line reference
- Troubleshooting common issues

**Contents**:
- Diagram type tags and syntax
- Sequence diagram elements (participants, arrows, grouping)
- Class diagram relationships and visibility
- ERD syntax and cardinality
- Activity diagram constructs
- Component and state diagrams
- Styling and theming options
- Creole text formatting essentials
- Layout control tricks
- Standard library imports
- Preprocessor features
- Output format commands
- Common troubleshooting

**When to use**: Keep this open while creating diagrams for quick syntax checks.

---

### 2. Creole Cheat Sheet (`creole-cheatsheet.md`)

**Purpose**: Complete reference for text formatting within PlantUML  
**Best for**:
- Formatting notes and labels
- Creating rich documentation in diagrams
- Building formatted legends and tables
- Adding visual emphasis to text

**Contents**:
- Basic text formatting (bold, italic, underline, etc.)
- HTML-style tags (colors, sizes, fonts)
- Lists (ordered and unordered)
- Tables with formatting
- Horizontal lines and separators
- Code blocks (inline and multi-line)
- Unicode symbols and special characters
- External and local file links
- Practical examples and patterns
- Tips and common gotchas

**When to use**: Reference when you need to format text in notes, create tables, or add visual styling to diagram content.

---

### 3. Standard Library Guide (`stdlib-guide.md`)

**Purpose**: Comprehensive guide to using PlantUML's icon libraries  
**Best for**:
- Cloud architecture diagrams (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Kubernetes and container diagrams
- Using professional icons in diagrams
- Technology stack visualization

**Contents**:
- Overview of available libraries
- AWS icons (Compute, Storage, Database, Networking, Security, etc.)
- Azure icons (with setup and examples)
- GCP icons
- Kubernetes sprites
- Font Awesome icons
- Material Design icons
- Office icons
- C4 Model components
- How to browse and discover icons
- Creating custom libraries
- Best practices and troubleshooting

**When to use**: Reference when building cloud architecture diagrams or when you need professional icons for technology components.

---

## How to Use These References

### During Diagram Creation

1. **Open Quick Reference** for basic syntax
2. **Reference Creole Cheat Sheet** when adding notes or documentation
3. **Check Standard Library Guide** when you need cloud provider or technology icons

### Learning PlantUML

1. Start with **Quick Reference** to understand basic structures
2. Practice with examples from each section
3. Experiment with **Creole formatting** to enhance readability
4. Explore **Standard Library** to make diagrams more professional

### As a Team Resource

- Share these references with team members
- Link from internal documentation
- Use as training materials for new team members
- Keep alongside diagram templates

## Quick Links by Use Case

### "I need to create a..."

- **Sequence diagram**: Quick Reference → Sequence Diagrams section
- **Database schema**: Quick Reference → Entity-Relationship Diagrams section
- **AWS architecture**: Standard Library Guide → AWS Icons section
- **Class model**: Quick Reference → Class Diagrams section
- **Formatted note**: Creole Cheat Sheet → Basic Text Formatting
- **Table in legend**: Creole Cheat Sheet → Tables section

### "How do I..."

- **Add colors**: Quick Reference → Styling section or Creole Cheat Sheet → Colors
- **Create lists**: Creole Cheat Sheet → Lists section
- **Use AWS icons**: Standard Library Guide → AWS Icons
- **Format code**: Creole Cheat Sheet → Code Blocks section
- **Control layout**: Quick Reference → Layout Control section
- **Add links**: Creole Cheat Sheet → Links section or Quick Reference → External Links

### "I want to..."

- **Make professional cloud diagrams**: Standard Library Guide
- **Style my diagrams consistently**: Quick Reference → Styling and Theming
- **Add rich text documentation**: Creole Cheat Sheet
- **Troubleshoot errors**: Quick Reference → Common Issues section
- **Use Kubernetes icons**: Standard Library Guide → Kubernetes section
- **Create reusable components**: Quick Reference → Preprocessor section

## Reference Format

All references follow a consistent format:

1. **Overview**: What the reference covers
2. **Syntax/Examples**: Concrete code examples
3. **Tables**: Quick lookup for options
4. **Practical Examples**: Real-world usage patterns
5. **Tips & Best Practices**: Expert guidance
6. **Common Issues**: Troubleshooting help

## Printing/PDF

These references are designed to be:
- **Printable**: Clear formatting for paper reference
- **Searchable**: Use Ctrl+F to find specific syntax
- **Linkable**: Share specific sections with team members
- **Standalone**: Each file is complete and independent

### Recommended Prints

For desk reference, consider printing:
- Quick Reference (most frequently used)
- Creole Cheat Sheet (if you add lots of notes)

## Contributing

### Adding New References

When creating new reference materials:

1. **Follow the format**: Match existing reference style
2. **Include examples**: Every syntax rule should have a working example
3. **Add tables**: Quick lookup tables are essential
4. **Test all code**: Verify all PlantUML examples render correctly
5. **Update this README**: Document the new reference

### Updating Existing References

- Keep PlantUML version compatibility in mind
- Note version-specific features
- Update "Last Updated" date
- Test examples with latest PlantUML version

## External Resources

These references complement but don't replace official documentation:

- **Official PlantUML Site**: https://plantuml.com/
- **PlantUML Online Editor**: http://www.plantuml.com/plantuml/
- **GitHub Repository**: https://github.com/plantuml/plantuml
- **Real World PlantUML**: https://real-world-plantuml.com/ (examples)
- **Standard Library Index**: https://plantuml.com/stdlib

## Feedback

Found an error or have a suggestion?
- Check against official PlantUML documentation
- Verify with latest PlantUML version
- Test your proposed change
- Submit update with working examples

---

**Reference Library Version**: 1.0  
**Last Updated**: February 2026  
**PlantUML Compatibility**: 1.2024.x and later
